# Committer Notes

{Please provide a brief description of what this PR accomplishes. Be sure to reference any issues addressed. If the PR is a work-in-progress submitted for early review, please include [WIP] at the beginning of the title or mark the PR as DRAFT.}

### All Submissions:

- [ ] If you are an external contributer, are you merging with a personal branch or `develop`?
- [ ] Have you checked to ensure there aren't other open [Pull Requests](https://github.com/brian-uf/oscal-class/pulls) for the same update/change?
- [ ] Have you squashed any non-relevant commits and commit messages? \[[instructions](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2/Git-Tools-Rewriting-History)\]

By submitting a pull request, you are agreeing to provide this contribution under the [MIT License](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).


### Changes to Core Features:

- [ ] Have you added an explanation of what your changes do and why you'd like to include them?
- [ ] Does the PR include new or updated unit tests for your core changes, as applicable?
- [ ] Have you added or updated documentation in the [docs](./docs) folder?
- [ ] Does the documenttion included examples of how to use your the feature(s)?
- [ ] Do all modules, functions and methods have docstrings suitable for use in documentation.
